_meta.name: Evidence-Based Therapeutic Frameworks
_meta.description: Established psychological, behavioural, and therapeutic frameworks for content creation
_meta.usage_note: These frameworks provide structural credibility. Apply brand voice, cognitive level, and proprietary philosophy on top.

kolb_experiential.name: Kolb's Experiential Learning Cycle
kolb_experiential.description: Learning happens through a cycle of experience, reflection, conceptualisation, and experimentation

stages.name: concrete_experience
stages.label: Concrete Experience
stages.description: Do the thing. Have the experience.

stages.name: reflective_observation
stages.label: Reflective Observation
stages.description: What happened? What did you notice?
stages.content_application: Reflection questions, journaling prompts, what-did-you-notice sections

stages.name: abstract_conceptualisation
stages.label: Abstract Conceptualisation
stages.description: What does this mean? What's the pattern?
stages.content_application: Psychoeducation, explaining the why, connecting to theory

stages.name: active_experimentation
stages.label: Active Experimentation
stages.description: How will you apply this? What will you try next?

structure.cycle_note: The cycle repeats - each round deepens skill

dreyfus_model.name: Dreyfus Model of Skill Acquisition
dreyfus_model.description: Five stages from beginner to expert - match content complexity to current stage

stages.name: novice
stages.label: Novice
stages.content_needs: Clear steps, explicit rules, simple frameworks

stages.name: competent
stages.label: Competent
stages.content_needs: Decision frameworks, prioritisation tools, complexity handling

dreyfus_model.application_note: Don't expect expert performance from novices. Match exercises to current stage.

deliberate_practice.name: Deliberate Practice
deliberate_practice.description: How skills are actually built at high levels - not mindless repetition but focused, feedback-rich practice

principles.name: specific_goals
principles.description: Focus on particular sub-skills, not vague improvement

principles.name: full_concentration
principles.description: Not mindless repetition - full attention required

principles.name: immediate_feedback
principles.description: Know if you're doing it right, adjust in real-time

principles.name: outside_comfort_zone
principles.description: Stretching, not coasting - working at the edge of ability

principles.name: repetition_with_refinement
principles.description: Do it again, better each time

deliberate_practice.application_note: Design exercises that follow these principles for maximum skill development

stages_of_change.name: Transtheoretical Model (Stages of Change)
stages_of_change.description: People move through predictable stages when changing behaviour - meet them where they are

stages.name: precontemplation
stages.label: Precontemplation
stages.content_approach: Raise awareness, plant seeds, no pressure

stages.name: contemplation
stages.label: Contemplation
stages.content_approach: Explore pros/cons, address ambivalence, build motivation

stages.name: preparation
stages.label: Preparation
stages.content_approach: Concrete planning, skill building, removing barriers

stages.name: maintenance
stages.label: Maintenance
stages.content_approach: Prevent relapse, build identity, long-term strategies

stages.name: relapse
stages.label: Relapse
stages.description: Part of the process for many
stages.content_approach: Normalise, learn from it, re-engage without shame

stages_of_change.application_note: A workbook for someone in contemplation looks different than one for someone in action

com_b.name: COM-B Model
com_b.description: Three conditions needed for behaviour change - diagnose why change isn't happening

components.name: capability
components.label: Capability
components.question: Can they do it? Do they have the skills and knowledge?

components.name: opportunity
components.label: Opportunity
components.question: Does the environment allow it? Do social norms support it?
components.content_intervention: Environment design, social support, removing barriers

components.name: motivation
components.label: Motivation
components.question: Do they want to? Is it rewarding?
components.content_intervention: Values connection, reward design, identity work

com_b.application_note: Use to diagnose why change isn't happening - is it capability, opportunity, or motivation?

implementation_intentions.name: Implementation Intentions
implementation_intentions.description: Specific if-then plans dramatically increase follow-through

structure.format: When [situation X occurs], I will [do behaviour Y]

components.name: situation
examples: When I sit down for dinner

components.name: behaviour
examples: I will drink a glass of water
I will take three deep breaths
I will put my phone in another room

implementation_intentions.application_note: All 'when will you do this' planning should use this format

habit_loop.name: Habit Loop
habit_loop.description: How habits form and can be changed - cue, craving, response, reward

components.name: cue
components.label: Cue
components.intervention: Make cues obvious (for good habits) or invisible (for bad habits)

components.name: craving
components.label: Craving
components.description: Motivation, the want behind the behaviour
components.intervention: Make it attractive - connect to identity, values, or pleasure

components.name: response
components.label: Response
components.intervention: Make it easy - reduce friction, start small

components.name: reward
components.label: Reward
components.description: The satisfaction that reinforces the habit
components.intervention: Make it satisfying - immediate reward, tracking, celebration

habit_loop.application_note: Design cues, make the behaviour easy, ensure reward is felt

smart_goals.name: SMART Goals
smart_goals.description: Framework for setting clear, achievable goals

criteria.name: Measurable
criteria.question: How will you know you've done it?

criteria.name: Achievable
criteria.question: Is this realistic for you right now?

criteria.name: Relevant
criteria.question: Does this matter to you? Does it connect to your values?

smart_goals.application_note: Often combined with Implementation Intentions for maximum effectiveness

woop.name: WOOP
woop.description: Mental contrasting with implementation intentions - more effective than positive visualisation alone

steps.name: Outcome
steps.description: What would be the best outcome? How would it feel?

woop.application_note: The obstacle step is key - this is what makes it more effective than just positive thinking

rain.name: RAIN
rain.description: Mindfulness-based approach to working with difficult emotions

steps.name: Recognise
steps.description: What is happening inside me right now?
steps.prompt: Notice the emotion, thought, or sensation

steps.name: Allow
steps.description: Can I let this be here?
steps.prompt: Don't try to fix or change it - just let it exist

steps.name: Investigate
steps.description: What does this feel like in my body? What does it need?

steps.name: Nurture
steps.description: What would I say to a friend feeling this?

structure.follow_up: After RAIN: Rest in awareness, notice any shift

rain.application_note: Useful for in-the-moment regulation and for processing after difficult experiences

window_of_tolerance.name: Window of Tolerance
window_of_tolerance.description: Understanding nervous system states and the optimal zone for functioning

structure.goal: Expand the window over time; return to the window when outside it

window_of_tolerance.application_note: Often combined with Polyvagal Theory for deeper understanding

cognitive_triangle.name: Cognitive Triangle
cognitive_triangle.description: The interconnection between thoughts, feelings, and behaviours

structure.key_insight: Change one, and the others shift. You can enter at any point.

abcde.name: ABCDE Model
abcde.description: Challenging unhelpful beliefs and building more helpful ones

steps.name: Belief
steps.description: What did you tell yourself about it?

steps.name: Consequence
steps.description: How did you feel? What did you do?

steps.name: Disputation
steps.description: Is that belief accurate? What's the evidence?

steps.name: Effect
steps.description: What's a more helpful way to see this? How does that feel?

gottman_four_horsemen.name: Gottman's Four Horsemen
gottman_four_horsemen.description: Four communication patterns that predict relationship failure, plus their antidotes

horsemen.name: criticism
horsemen.example: You never think about anyone but yourself
horsemen.antidote: Gentle startup - express need without blame

horsemen.name: contempt
horsemen.antidote: Build culture of appreciation and respect

horsemen.name: defensiveness
horsemen.example: That's not my fault, you're the one who...
horsemen.antidote: Take responsibility, even for a small part

horsemen.name: stonewalling
horsemen.description: Withdrawing, shutting down, refusing to engage
horsemen.example: Walking away, silent treatment, zoning out
horsemen.antidote: Self-soothe, take a break, then return

nvc.name: Nonviolent Communication
nvc.description: Framework for compassionate communication that meets needs

steps.name: observations
steps.example: When I see dishes in the sink for three days...

steps.name: feelings
steps.description: What do you feel about it?

steps.name: needs
steps.description: What need is connected to that feeling?
steps.example: Because I need order and shared responsibility...

steps.name: requests
steps.description: What would you like? (Request, not demand)
steps.example: Would you be willing to wash them tonight?

attachment_styles.name: Attachment Styles
attachment_styles.description: Patterns of relating based on early experience

styles.name: secure
styles.in_relationships: Can ask for needs, tolerate conflict, trust others

styles.name: anxious
styles.in_relationships: Needs closeness, worries about rejection, may seem 'clingy'

styles.name: avoidant
styles.in_relationships: Pulls away when things get close, may seem 'distant'

styles.name: disorganised
styles.description: Mixed/contradictory patterns, wants closeness but fears it
styles.in_relationships: Push-pull dynamic, difficulty with consistent relating

structure.key_insight: Attachment styles can change with awareness and secure relationships

relapse_prevention.name: Relapse Prevention
relapse_prevention.description: Framework for maintaining change and handling setbacks

components.name: lifestyle_balance
components.description: Build a life that supports the change

components.name: lapse_vs_relapse
components.description: A slip doesn't equal failure (abstinence violation effect)

components.name: recovery_plan
components.description: What to do if you slip - get back on track without catastrophising

halt.name: HALT
halt.description: Simple check for vulnerability states - address basic needs first

letters.name: Hungry
letters.check: Have I eaten? Is my blood sugar stable?

letters.name: Angry
letters.check: Am I holding onto resentment or frustration?

letters.name: Lonely
letters.check: Have I connected with anyone? Am I isolating?

letters.name: Tired
letters.check: Have I slept? Am I rested?

structure.principle: When you notice these, you're more vulnerable. Address basic needs first.

wrap.name: Wellness Recovery Action Plan (WRAP)
wrap.description: Structured self-management system for mental health

sections.name: wellness_toolbox
sections.description: What helps you stay well - your resources

sections.name: daily_maintenance
sections.description: What you do every day to stay on track

sections.name: triggers
sections.description: What sets you off, and how to respond

sections.name: crisis_plan
sections.description: What to do in emergency - who to call, what helps

values_card_sort.name: Values Card Sort
values_card_sort.description: Process for identifying and prioritising personal values

process.action: Rank or prioritise the final values
Reflect on what these mean and how you live them

wheel_of_life.name: Wheel of Life
wheel_of_life.description: Visual assessment of life balance across key areas

process.action: Rate each area 1-10 for current satisfaction
Plot on wheel - visualise the shape

polyvagal.name: Polyvagal Theory
polyvagal.description: Three-part nervous system explains stress responses and the path to safety

states.name: ventral_vagal
states.label: Ventral Vagal (Safe & Social)
states.access_to: Full range of responses, social engagement

structure.key_insight: We move between states based on neuroception (unconscious safety scanning). The goal is building capacity to return to ventral.

polyvagal.application_note: Often combined with Window of Tolerance for comprehensive nervous system education

hermans_phases.name: Herman's Three Stages of Trauma Recovery
hermans_phases.description: Phased approach to trauma recovery - safety first

phases.name: Safety & Stabilisation
phases.focus: Establishing safety, stabilising symptoms, building resources

phases.name: Remembrance & Mourning
phases.requires: Sufficient safety and stabilisation from Phase 1

phases.name: Reconnection
phases.focus: Rebuilding life, reclaiming identity, reconnecting with others

structure.key_principle: Never skip Phase 1. Safety must come first.

hermans_phases.application_note: Aligns with proprietary 'Resilience Before Processing' principle

grounding_categories.name: Grounding Techniques
grounding_categories.description: Categories of techniques for returning to present-moment awareness

categories.name: sensory
categories.description: Using the five senses to anchor in the present

skill_building.name: Skill Building
skill_building.description: Frameworks for learning and developing new skills

behaviour_change.name: Behaviour Change
behaviour_change.description: Frameworks for understanding and facilitating behaviour change

goal_setting.name: Goal Setting & Planning
goal_setting.description: Frameworks for setting and achieving goals

cognitive_work.name: Cognitive Work
cognitive_work.description: Frameworks for understanding and changing thought patterns

relationships.name: Relationships
relationships.description: Frameworks for understanding and improving relationships

recovery.name: Recovery & Maintenance
recovery.description: Frameworks for maintaining change and managing setbacks

self_discovery.name: Self-Discovery
self_discovery.description: Frameworks for self-understanding and values clarification

combining_frameworks.description: Many workbooks use multiple frameworks together

framework_vs_workbook.description: The framework provides structure. You still need:

citation_by_cognitive_level.level_2: Named attribution: 'This approach is based on [framework] developed by [source]'
citation_by_cognitive_level.level_3: Light touch: 'Research shows...' or 'One helpful framework...'
citation_by_cognitive_level.level_4: Implicit: Use the structure without naming it
